scope.summary: Implement a parameterized built-in avatar library with seeded body-type archetypes (female/male, petite/standard/tall, straight/hourglass/athletic) driven by anthropometric measurement sliders that write to tailor_avatars + tailor_body_proxies authority rows
acceptance_criteria: At least 6 seeded named body-type archetypes exist as authority rows in tailor_avatars at migration time (female_petite, female_standard, female_tall, male_standard, male_athletic, non_binary_androgynous)
Each archetype row carries a 25-field measurements_mm_json map and a linked BPX- capsule proxy row
A slider-style update API accepts normalized [0,1] per-measurement delta and re-emits TailorAvatarMeasurementsExtracted with the adjusted map
The updated measurements_mm_json and linked capsule chain (recomputed via compute_capsule_chain) are stored without manual BPX- authoring
TailorAvatarCreated and TailorBodyProxyCreated are emitted for seeded rows during migration; TailorAvatarMeasurementsExtracted is emitted on slider update
Cargo test tailor_avatar_library::test_slider_bust_update passes: bust_circ_mm changes propagate to capsule radius within 5 mm
scope.risk_if_missed: No default body exists for first-time operators or model sessions. Every garment author must supply a custom avatar before the first simulation run, creating high friction and breaking the model-first onboarding path.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
